description: This schematic illustration depicts the evolution of inclusions during the smelting and solidification processes in Fe-15Mn-xAl-C low-density steels with varying aluminum contents (1 wt%, 3 wt%, and 5 wt% Al). The left panel shows the initial Al₂O₃ inclusions formed during smelting, appearing as black elongated polygons that decrease in abundance as Al content increases from 1% to 5%. The central section illustrates the precipitation sequence at the liquidus temperature, where AlN (gray hexagons), MnS (gray irregular shapes), and TiN (orange angular forms) precipitate during solidification in the order AlN > MnS > TiN. The right panel displays the final composite inclusions formed through heterogeneous nucleation, where early precipitates provide nucleation sites for later ones, creating complex multi-layer structures including Al₂O₃-MnS, AlN-MnS, TiN-MnS, and Al₂O₃-AlN-MnS. The figure demonstrates that increasing Al content reduces single-particle Al₂O₃ formation, advances AlN precipitation when increasing from 1% to 3% Al but delays it at 5% Al, and consistently delays MnS and TiN precipitation, thereby altering the solidification route and final inclusion morphologies.
